channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For March 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 19, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Fashion
  • Hint 2: Stage
  • Hint 3: Runway

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Supermodel
  • Grace
  • Strut
  • Glamour
  • Backstage
  • Menswear
  • Womenswear
  • Fashion
  • Runway

Today’s Contexto Answer For March 19, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Catwalk

A Catwalk is a narrow walkway, along which models often walk to display clothing. Also used as open bridges in industrial installations.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 20.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Meta Horizon Worlds, a VR App, is Leaving VR to Become ‘Mobile Only’
item.content:encoded:
Meta has revealed that Horizon Worlds, a VR-exclusive application built for Meta Quest headsets, will start being removed from the VR ecosystem as of March 31. Horizon Worlds and Events will no longer surface in the Quest Store, and Central, the Events Arena, Kaiju, and Bobber Bay worlds will be stripped from the platform.

On June 15, the entire Horizon Worlds application will disappear from the VR space forever, being transformed instead into a mobile experience contained within the Meta Horizon mobile app.

Why is Horizon Worlds Being Removed?

In a post on the Meta community blog, the team wrote:

Earlier this year, we shared an update on our renewed focus for VR and Horizon. We are separating the two platforms so each can grow with greater focus, and the Horizon Worlds platform will become a mobile-only experience. This separation will extend across our ecosystem, including our mobile app.

To support this vision, we are making the following changes to streamline your Quest experience throughout 2026.

The ‘following changes’ went on to schedule the removal of the entire Horizon Worlds app from the Quest platform. It was also confirmed that the removal of Horizon Worlds will have a knock-on effect on Horizon+, the Quest’s premium subscription service, reducing its value a little.

This news has some fans understandably concerned, as in the last few months, some of the VR studios under the Meta label were shut down, and more than a few projects were cancelled. This even impacted some top-tier VR developers, like Twisted Pixel, the team that made Deadpool VR.

Are you sad to see the back of Horizon Worlds? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Pearl Abyss’ share price has plummeted

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Crimson Desert Devs Share Price Crashes Following Review Scores Reveal
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has suffered a 29% plunge in share value as the review scores for Crimson Desert emerge online. Earlier reports indicated that shareholders anticipated drastically high scores, as high as 90 out of 100 from aggregators, but the reality is a little further down the scale.

In a financial report, it was revealed that, on the South Korean stock market, Pearl Abyss was trading at 46,600 won, down 28.9% from the previous session, before review scores had been posted.

Crimson Desert Might Be All Style and No Substance

At the time of writing, Crimson Desert was staring down a Metacritic rating of 78, which is a far cry from the 90 expected by internal teams and shareholders. The scale for Crimson Desert is more diverse than most games, with everything from 10/10 to 4/10 scores having been handed down by critics.

It’s rare to see a game emerge with such a divisive nature, and that’s having a knock-on impact on Pearl Abyss’ share value:

In our review, we awarded Crimson Desert a 7/10, and our critic, Charlie Champion, wrote:

Crimson Desert is a game of extremes. At its best, it offers a breathtaking world filled with meaningful exploration, impressive scale, and genuinely thrilling battles. However, that brilliance is occasionally weighed down by an overabundance of unfocused systems, a weak narrative, and combat that struggles to maintain consistency.

In a report from the Seoul Economic Daily, it was noted that investors were ‘dumping stock’ following ‘disappointing reviews.’ Thanks to Shinobi on social media for that spot.

Crimson Desert officially launches today, March 19, but some prospective fans are now sceptical about the game’s power to deliver. It has spent weeks under intense scrutiny, with some stating that the title looks ‘too good to be true’, and now that reviews are emerging, that might be the case.

Are you still excited to get your hands on Crimson Desert? Let us know what you’re saying on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Aspyr has denied using AI in Tomb Raider

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Overwatch Co-Creator Jokes You Need ‘8 Billion Players’ To Succeed in Free-to-Play
item.content:encoded:
Jeff Kaplan saw Overwatch transition to a free-to-play model, so when he talks about what it takes to make such a concept succeed, you’d best believe he knows what he’s talking about. In a recent super-sized stream, Kaplan was playing his new game, The Legend of California, and he was asked if the game would be free to play at launch.

With a tone that was joking, but bordering on being serious, Kaplan explained what you need to make free-to-play games a success, and it involves having ‘billions of players’.

‘That’s Not Us’

The Legend of California has been turning heads recently, emerging as a solid-looking open-world action-survival game with arcade-based Red Dead Redemption vibes. It’s a far cry from Overwatch, which is what Jeff Kaplan is perhaps best known for.

Many have dubbed it ‘Rust but Wild West’, and it’s easy to see why.

Kaplan is leading the charge on The Legend of California, and he recently appeared on a ten-hour stream playing the game and answering questions. One popped up about the game’s intended monetization model and whether it would be free-to-play, to which Kaplan answered:

In game cash… I have no idea. I don’t know what the business model is.

I don’t think we’re going to be free-to-play. To be free-to-play, you need like eight billion players and two thousand devs cranking out f***ing keychains like a sweatshop.

That’s not us, we’re not going to be able to support the game on free-to-play. I’d be happy to be proven wrong on that.

I want to do something simple.

(Credit to Majid Manzarpour)

Kaplan went on to explain that you unlock ‘stuff’ (meaning cosmetics and customization options) by just playing the game, which is the way most players would have it, if they could.

All this was captured during the same stream in which Kaplan made a bold suggestion. He recommended that folks bagging on games they had no intention of playing should ‘shut the f*** up.’

The Legend of California looks great, but it’s in its early days. The team at Kintsugiyama numbers just 34, and it’s a rudimentary setup that’s evolving alongside the game.

Do you think The Legend of California looks like your kind of game? Let us know what you think on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Aspyr Denies Using AI To Create ‘Awful’ Tomb Raider Outfits
item.content:encoded:
Last week, the Tomb Raider Remastered collection received an update dubbed Challenge Mode, which came hand in hand with a slew of new outfits that fans had huge issues with. Aside from being generally awful and low-quality, many believed that they were created with generative AI.

The update caused the game’s review score to plummet, with 71% of the most recent reviews on Steam alone being firmly negative. In a statement, Aspyr has addressed the concerns of players, suggesting that no AI-generated assets were used in the update.

AI or Not, They’re Pretty Terrible

In a recent statement published on social media, the team at Aspyr wrote:

We hear and appreciate your feedback regarding the recent Challenge Mode update for Tomb Raider I-III Remastered. Our top priority is the delivery of a patch that will fix the texture issues and technical bugs.

In addition, a series of updates are on the way to address a variety of technical issues across all platforms.

This is also an opportunity to correct some mistaken information: The outfits in the update were created by our team of artists; no AI generated assets were used in the update.

We’ll release more details on the patch content and timing as soon as possible.

(Thanks to PCGamer for the spot)

In the immediate comments under the statement, fans flocked with counter-claims, with many pointing out that the key art used for the patch itself was obviously AI-generated.

The recent reviews on Steam represent a damning indictment of the situation.

One user wrote, ‘Insulting not only the customers, but even the game devs by adding AI garbage? Shame on you Aspyr. How dare you?’

Another wrote, ‘I don’t want to play Slop Raider. Can you please provide a pre-challenge mode version of the game via beta branch?’

It has been claimed extensively that the Challenge Mode update has effectively broken the game in several ways, which is what Aspyr was alluding to in the recent statement posted above.

Do you think generative AI was used in the recent Tomb Raider update? Let us know what you think on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1012)—Answers for Thursday, March 19
item.content:encoded:
You’ve almost made it through another week, but before you hit the weekend, you’ve got a couple more NYT Connections puzzles to complete. Today’s isn’t all that much of a barrier, but I’m still here with the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to get this challenge completed as quickly as possible.

How’s your streak going? Let me know in the comments.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (March 19)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • BABY BLUES
  • EVIL EYE
  • MOOD RING
  • PET ROCK
  • TRAFFIC LIGHT
  • CHICKEN LITTLE
  • SCRAP METAL
  • HORSESHOE
  • GOLDILOCKS
  • RABBIT’S FOOT
  • CHAMELEON
  • SODA POP
  • SUNSET
  • GINGERBREAD MAN
  • FOUR-LEAF CLOVER
  • FROG PRINCE

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Iconic folk and fairy tale characters
  • Green – Things that might bring you luck
  • Blue – Things that you’d see changing color
  • Purple – Terms that end with a genre of music

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, March 19:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, March 19 (#1012)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – CHICKEN LITTLE, FROG PRINCE, GINGERBREAD MAN, GOLDILOCKS
  • Green – EVIL EYE, FOUR-LEAF CLOVER, HORSESHOE, RABBIT’S FOOT
  • Blue – CHAMELEON, MOOD RING, SUNSET, TRAFFIC LIGHT
  • Purple – BABY BLUES, PET ROCK, SCRAP METAL, SODA POP

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up? Fortnite March 19 Downtime, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite players around the world have had their play sessions disrupted by server downtime, with the Epic Games title set to be unavailable for several hours on March 19.

Server downtime is a regular occurrence for live-service titles like Fortnite, though that doesn’t make it any less frustrating if it disrupts your plans.

If you’re wondering what’s going on with the Fortnite servers on March 19 and want to know when Fortnite will be playable again, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

Fortnite servers are down for several hours for the start of Chapter 7, Season 2, though Epic Games has seemingly confirmed exactly when servers are expected to be back up.

Downtime for the launch of Chapter 7, Season 2 begins at 2 AM ET, and an in-game countdown indicates downtime is expected to last for around five hours.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is There a Live Event For Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2?

The countdown is due to end at 7 AM ET, indicating that this is when players should expect the doors to open for the new season.

However, the times may still be subject to change, particularly if there any issues that arise while the patch is being deployed. The best place to keep an eye on the server status is social media.

What’s In The Fortnite March 19 Update?

The March 19 Fortnite update is the biggest in a long while, finally marking the start of Chapter 7, Season 2.

Initially due to release on March 5, Epic Games delayed the start of the new season by two weeks, making Chapter 7, Season 1, the third-longest on record.

The return of The Seven continues, with a head-to-head showdown against the Ice King, and artwork for the new season reveals Bugs Bunny is among those set to star. Other Looney Tunes characters are expected in the shop.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Register For Fortnite Save The World

A teaser for the new season also showed several characters frozen by the Ice King, including Captain America, opening the door for another potential Marvel collab.

As usual, the new season will see a sweep of changes to the Battle Royale landscape with new POIs, weapons, bosses, and more.

What are you hoping to see in Fortnite Chapter 7, Season 2? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, read about the return to Google Play Store, and check out the teaser of a Super Meat Boy 3D crossover.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Strands #745 Hints and Answers for Today, March 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #745 for Wednesday, March 18, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 18, 2026

The Strands’ answers to Game #745’s theme are “It follows.” So think about what follows life in your answers.

General Strands Hints For March 18

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: _____, sweat, and tears.
  • Hint 2: Teachers plan these individual subjects daily.
  • Hint 3: Fashion is all about one thing.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #745 for March 18, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 18, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • LIFE
  • PLEA
  • REACH
  • FLOOD
  • LESS

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #745 On March 18, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • COACH
  • LESSON
  • PRESERVER
  • STYLE
  • CYCLE
  • BLOOD
  • SPANAGRAM: AFTERLIFE

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #745?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 19 will be Strands Game #746.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-745-hints-and-answers-for-today-march-18-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Who Resident Evil’s Leon Kennedy is Married to Will One Day Be Clear
item.content:encoded:
In Resident Evil Requiem, concept art depicts Leon Kennedy wearing a wedding band. But just who is he married to? The game’s director isn’t ready to say just yet.

Resident Evil Requiem has captivated audiences worldwide. It’s become the fastest-selling game in the franchise and one of the best-rated games of the year so far.

That’s due in no small part to the inclusion of Leon Kennedy, the fan-favorite RE protagonist, who was one of the main characters in this year’s release and had a literal ax to grind. Still, it appears he also had a major reason to fight, a loving partner to go home to.

The Answer Will Become Clear One Day, Says Resident Evil Requiem Director

Resident Evil Requiem director Koshi Nakanishi told Eurogamer that one day the answer would become clear, but they wanted the audience to know, in the game’s final scene, that Leon did have a place to go home. The director also says he hopes audiences imagine Leon being at peace at home

“Leon is a man who says very little about the things that truly matter, but he would sacrifice himself without a second thought to save a life right in front of him. So what does his ring signify? Resolve? Or a vow?

“He may never voice the answer himself, but for now, after finishing a long battle, he has a ‘place to go home to’. Isn’t that enough?”

In the lead-up to the game’s release, Nakanishi spoke about the importance of the audience recognizing Leon’s changed demeanor. He was more aggressive in this release, and it was important to convey that. It also appears important to convey that there was hope in Leon’s life.

Did you play Resident Evil requiem? What do you think Leon’s ring signifies? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/who-resident-evils-leon-kennedy-is-married-to-will-one-day-be-clear/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: EXCLUSIVE – PlayStation is Phasing Out The “PlayStation Network” Branding
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation will be dropping the “PlayStation Network” and “PSN” branding by September 2026.

In an email sent to developers and seen by Insider Gaming, PlayStation will be dropping the “PlayStation Network” and “PSN” branding. It’s said that the change will be purely visual and is a means to “properly capture the breadth of our evolving digital services.”

The full email reads,

“We’d like to inform you that Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) has strategically decided to phase out the terms “PlayStation Network” and “PSN” across our platform in order to properly capture the breadth of our evolving digital services. What’s changing and timeline.

The upcoming changes are purely visual and will not introduce any technical alterations to our offerings. To simplify and unify branding, the terms “PlayStation Network” and “PSN” will be phased out across all SIE assets by September 2026. All features currently associated with PSN, including core network features such as friends, multiplayer, and trophies, will remain unaffected and available to players. You’ll be notified ahead of changes coinciding with the Technical Requirements Checklist (TRC) update in the fall of 2026.

Note: PS5 DevNet access is required to view TRC documentation. At that time, you’ll need to align with the updated TRC and branding guidelines to ensure the removal of PSN instances from all future releases, assets, and external service interfaces.”

It’s currently unclear on what the new name of the network will be.

What do you think of PlayStation dropping the “PlayStation Network” and “PSN” branding? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Gray Zone Warfare’s 0.4 update is coming soon

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.
